observations.recommendation:
6.13 We recommended and management agreed to comply with the following
audit recommendations:

a) Municipal Treasurer (i) continue furnishing the Municipal Accountant
at the beginning of each year with a certified list of taxpayers and
the amounts due and collectible during the year, which will serve as the basis
for setting up the RPT Receivables; and (ii) fully update the RPTAR to
ensure the accuracy of the total amount of delinquencies;
b) Municipal Accountant prepare and maintain the required Subsidiary
Ledgers (SLs) for Real Property Tax and Special Education Tax
Receivables, in accordance with Section 4, Chapter 2, Volume I of the
NGAS Manual for LGUs. These ledgers should provide a breakdown of
receivables per taxpayer to support the General Ledger balances; and
c) Municipal Treasurer and Accountant completely reconcile their
respective records to identify and resolve any discrepancies in the
reported balances of RPT and SET receivables and their reciprocal
accounts.

7.17 We recommended and the: a) Municipal Accountant agreed to continuously
coordinate with the MSWDO to establish the collectability of the Loans Receivables-
Others; and b) MSWDO agreed to exert effort for the possible collection of some
loans.

7.18 We also recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to expedite the
preparation and completion required documents to support the request for
authority to write-off dormant receivable accounts, and thereafter, file the request for
the approval of COA, pursuant to the provisions COA Circular No. 2023- 008.

8.4 We recommended and the LCE agreed to:
(a) direct the GSO and the Inventory Committee to: (i) conduct a complete
and timely physical count of all inventory items in accordance with
Section 124 of the NGAS Manual for LGUs; and (ii) prepare and submit
the resulting Report on the Physical Count of Inventories (RPCI) within
the prescribed deadlines, ensuring that it accurately reflects only the
inventory items physically on hand at year-end;
(b) require all offices with custody of inventories to: (i) maintain the
mandated Stock Cards (SCs) for each inventory item; (ii) implement a
standardized and complete inventory documentation system, including
the recording of receipts, issuances, consumptions, and balances of
inventory items; and (iii) Submit monthly Requisition Issue Slips (RIS)
and Summary of Supplies and Materials Issued (SSMI) reflecting the
movement and balances of inventory items;
(c) instruct the GSO to consolidate the submitted RISs and SSMIs and
transmit them to the Accounting Office promptly to facilitate timely and
accurate recording of inventory transactions in the books of accounts;
(d) direct the Municipal Accountant to consistently update the Supplies
Ledger Cards (SLCs) to ensure accurate accounting records; and
(e) instruct the Accounting Office and GSO to coordinate regularly and
perform periodic reconciliation of their respective inventory records to
ensure consistency and accuracy of reported inventory balances.

9.7 We recommended, and the LCE agreed to instruct the:
a) Inventory Committee to submit the revised RPCPPE duly providing all
the necessary information, as discussed; and
b) GSO and Disposal Committee to (i) expedite the request for the timely
disposal of unserviceable PPE items to prevent further loss and
deterioration of the same; and (ii) submit the IIRUP together with the
necessary supporting documents for review and verification by the Audit
Team.

9.8 We also recommended and management agreed that the Municipality
consider establishing a mutually agreed policy or agreement, with the DepEd Tarlac
Province Division Office, on managing SEF property; or transferring, by way of
donation through Sangguniang Bayan Resolution, to the DepEd, the SEF-funded
properties which are movable and intended for use directly in the classroom so that
the person in actual physical possession/control of the SEF property will be made
accountable for its proper use and safekeeping.

9.9 Lastly, we recommended and management agreed to (i) allocate budget for
insurance policies covering all serviceable motor vehicles and all insurable
properties to secure said policies from the GSIS so that the agency is protected and
indemnified should disasters arise; and (ii) prepare PIF to be submitted to GSIS and
Audit Team not later than April 30 of each year, pursuant to COA Circular No.
2018-002.

10.7 We recommended and management agreed to comply with the following
audit recommendations:

a) Municipal Accountant (i) adopt a methodology based on historical data
to estimate the portion of leave credits expected to be used or paid within
12 months and classify them as current liabilities, and the remaining
accumulated leave benefits, expected to be payable upon separation, be
recognized as long-term liabilities in compliance with IPSAS 39; and (ii)
recognize leave benefits yearly as they are earned, rather than recording
the entire liability in a single year;
b) Municipal Budget Officer establish an Employee Benefits Reserve Fund
on a yearly basis to ensure sufficient cash reserves for future
monetization; and
c) Human Resource Department coordinate with the Municipal Accounting
Office and Municipal Budget Office to ensure adherence to Rule XVI of the
Omnibus Rules Implementing Book V of Executive Order (EO) No.
292 on the yearly accounting and funding of leave benefits.

11.11 We recommended and the Accounting Office agreed to, moving forward,
and in light of the insufficient guidelines, record the twenty percent (20%) BFP Fire
Code Fee share under the GF rather than TF. Furthermore, the utilization of the
said fund should undergo the regular appropriation process and obtain approval
from the local Sanggunian.

12.11 We recommended and the Municipal Treasurer agreed to (i) continue to
enforce compliance with the Municipal Revenue Code to ensure the correct business
tax accrues to the Municipality; (ii) ensure that businesses consistently submit the
required sworn statements of their gross sales/receipts and copies of income tax
returns to guarantee the reliability and accuracy of the reported taxable bases; and
(iii) ensure that the required stratified schedule of presumptive income levels is
prepared every tax period to substantiate the estimated gross receipts of each
business classification.

13.7 We recommended and management agreed to (i) continue to implement
LGSF-SBDP projects to bring development to conflict-affected barangays; and (ii)
post the necessary reports on the LGU’s website and full disclosure boards to keep
their constituents informed of how the funds are managed, disbursed and used.

14.14 We recommended and management agreed to (i) continue to ensure that the
required appropriation for local development fund is always observed and (ii)
continue to comply with the guidelines mandated by DBM-DOF-DILG JMC No.
2020-1 in the utilization of the 20% DF.

14.15 Also, we recommended and management agreed that the Municipal
Development Council adhere to the guidelines set by COA MC No. 95-216 in the
preparation of the AIP and Annual Budget.

15.16 We recommended and management agreed to comply with the following
audit recommendations:

a) MDRRMO and MSWDO (i) prepare and submit monthly RISs and
SSMIs to the Accounting Office to facilitate accurate recognition of
expenses for items already consumed and/or distributed, and (ii)
maintain complete and updated inventory records to properly monitor
the receipt and distribution of medical supplies and welfare goods;
b) Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) strictly comply with the
requirements of R.A. No. 9184, its Revised IRR, and COA Circular Nos.
2012-001 and 2023-004 by securing and maintaining all necessary
procurement documents; and
c) Accounting Office (i) ensure the completeness of documents before
processing DVs for payment and (ii) submit to the Audit Team the DVs
pertaining to the noted transactions above.

16.10 We recommended and management agreed to comply with the following
audit recommendations:

a) GFPS (i) submit in writing the changes in the DILG-approved GPB and
secure an acknowledgment receipt of adjusted GPB from the DILG to
ensure the compliance of their adjustments to the requirements of the
JMCs; and (ii) facilitate the necessary training to enhance familiarity
and capacity in administering the HGDG and PIMME tools and
implement the use of these tools for effective planning, implementation,
and monitoring of GAD PPAs; and
b) Municipal Accountant submit to the Audit Team the DVs pertaining to
the noted transactions above.

17.11 We recommended and management agreed to comply with the following
audit recommendations:

a) LSB (i) submit proof that a three-year program was formulated as
required by the JC; (ii) ensure the future distribution of the copy of the
SEF Budget to the Municipal Budget Officer, Municipal Accountant,
and Municipal Treasurer be promptly accomplished and shall be
supported with proper transmittal records from the concerned
departments; and (iii) comply with requirements of the JC on the
submission of quarterly reports and annual reports on SEF utilization to
the relevant agencies; and
b) LCE instruct personnel to promptly update the postings of the SEF
Utilization Report in the three (3) conspicuous places and ensure that the
said reports will be posted in the Municipality’s website.

We recommended and the Local Chief Executive (LCE) agreed to instruct the
a) Municipal Treasurer to furnish the Municipal Accountant at the beginning of the year a certified list of taxpayers and the amount due and collectible during the year which will serve as basis of the latter in setting-up the RPT Receivables at the beginning of the year;
b) Municipal Accountant prepare the corresponding subsidiary ledgers required in Volume I of the MNGAS for LGUs; and
c) Municipal Treasurer and Accountant reconcile their records to come up with the correct balances of the RPT/SET Receivables and their corresponding reciprocal accounts.

We further recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to
(i) take up the necessary adjusting/correcting entries on the errors made in the recording of the collections of advance RPT and SET payments; and
(ii) ensure that accounting procedures in recording RPT transactions are consistent with the provisions of IPSAS 23 and MNGAS for LGUs.

We reiterated the audit recommendation and the Municipal Accountant agreed to exhaust all remedies to gather the supporting documents and details of the Due from LGUs, NGAs and NGOs/POs accounts, and the unaccounted portion of Other Receivables account.

We recommended and Management agreed to exert effort to recover the unsettled cash advances totaling ₱1,343,785.16 from the two individuals for the account recorded as Other Receivables.

We reiterated the audit recommendations and the LCE agreed to instruct the Accounting Office to
(i) coordinate with the Municipal Social Welfare Development Office for the establishment of the collectability of the Loans Receivables and record adjustments, if any; and
(ii) consider the write-off of dormant accounts in accordance with the procedures outlined in COA Circular 2016-005.

We recommended and the LCE agreed to instruct the
a) Accounting Office to coordinate with the Municipal Assessor’s Office for the proper set-up, maintenance, and updating of the SLs and record necessary adjusting entries, if any; and
b) Assessor’s Office to conduct collection campaign through sending of reminder/demand letters to the delinquent beneficiaries.

We recommended and Management agreed that
(a) GSO and Inventory Committee (i) conduct the periodic physical count of inventories and
(ii) prepare and submit the RPCI to the Audit Team;
(b) GSO maintain the SCs to record receipts and issuances of supplies; and
(c) Municipal Accountant maintain the SLCs for each type of supplies.

We recommended and Management, headed by the LCE, agreed to continue to:
(i) ensure optimal use of the DF so that socio-economic programs and projects that are beneficial to the LGU’s constituents are timely implemented; and
(ii) comply with the rules on the appropriation and utilization of the 20% DF for eligible projects pursuant to Item 3.2.5 of the DBM-DOF-DILG JMC No. 1 dated November 4, 2020.

We reiterated the audit recommendation and the LCE agreed to direct the Municipal Planning and Development Office (MPDO) and Municipal Budget Office (MBO) to assist the Municipal Development Council in adhering to the guidelines set by COA MC No. 95-216 in preparing the AIP and Annual Budget.

We recommended and the LCE agreed to direct the
(a) the Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) to (i) strictly follow the provisions of the IRR of R.A. No. 9184 in the procurement of goods and services; and (ii) justify the deviation from pertinent guidelines in the procurement of the rescue vehicle and heavy equipment; and
(b) Municipal Accountant to check the completeness of supporting documents provided by COA Circular 2012-001 before effecting payment for contracted projects.

We further recommend the submission of the revised AIP as required by NDRRMC, DBM, and DILG JMC 2013-1 dated March 25, 2013.

We recommended and Management agreed to instruct the
(a) GFPS to (i) update the Municipality’s GAD Database; (ii) submit in writing the changes in the DILG-approved GPB and secure an acknowledgment receipt of adjusted GPB from the DILG to ensure the compliance of their adjustments to the requirements of the JMCs; and (iii) implement the use of the HGDG and PIMME tools to measure the actual cost attributed to gender-responsiveness and know if the existing mechanism actually responds to the basic 5% requirement of the law; and
(b) Municipal Accountant submit to the Audit Team the DVs pertaining to the noted transactions above.

We recommended and the LCE agreed to direct the Municipal Treasurer to
(i) continue to comply and enforce the Municipal Revenue Code so that correct business tax will accrue for the benefit of the Municipality; and
(ii) require the submission of a sworn statement of the business’ gross sales/receipt and copy of income tax return to ensure that the basis declared by the taxpayers are reliable and accurate.

We recommended and Management agreed to make representation or send communication to the DENR-EMB regarding the status or condition of the transferred equipment and facility to the Municipality in order to undertake necessary measures, either to repair or return them, to stop the risks of their further deterioration and wastage of government funds.

We also recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to record the biodegradable waste shredder and composter totaling ₱1,005,000.00, which were transferred to the Municipality, in the books of accounts.

We recommended and Management agreed to instruct the (a) GFPS to (i) update the Municipality’s GAD Database; (ii) submit in writing the changes in the DILG-approved GPB and secure an acknowledgment receipt of adjusted GPB from the DILG to ensure the compliance of their adjustments to the requirements of the JMCs; and (iii) implement the use of the HGDG and PIMME tools to measure the actual cost attributed to gender-responsiveness and know if the existing mechanism actually responds to the basic 5% requirement of the law; and (b) Municipal Accountant submit to the Audit Team the DVs pertaining to the noted transactions above.

6.17 We recommended and the Local Chief Executive (LCE) agreed to instruct
the a) Municipal Treasurer to furnish the Municipal Accountant at the beginning of
the year a certified list of taxpayers and the amount due and collectible during the
year which will serve as basis of the latter in setting-up the RPT Receivables at the
beginning of the year; b) Municipal Accountant prepare the corresponding
subsidiary ledgers required in Volume I of the MNGAS for LGUs; and c) Municipal
Treasurer and Accountant reconcile their records to come up with the correct
balances of the RPT/SET Receivables and their corresponding reciprocal accounts.
6.18 We further recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to (i) take
up the necessary adjusting/correcting entries on the errors made in the recording of
the collections of advance RPT and SET payments; and (ii) ensure that accounting
procedures in recording RPT transactions are consistent with the provisions of
IPSAS 23 and MNGAS for LGUs.

7.9 We reiterated the audit recommendation and the Municipal Accountant
agreed to exhaust all remedies to gather the supporting documents and details of the
Due from LGUs, NGAs and NGOs/POs accounts, and the unaccounted portion of
Other Receivables account.
7.10 We recommended and Management agreed to exert effort to recover the
unsettled cash advances totaling ₱1,343,785.16 from the two individuals for the
account recorded as Other Receivables.

8.9 We reiterated the audit recommendations and the LCE agreed to instruct the
Accounting Office (i) to coordinate with the Municipal Social Welfare Development
Office for the establishment of the collectability of the Loans Receivables and record
adjustments, if any; and (ii) consider the write-off of dormant accounts in
accordance with the procedures outlined in COA Circular 2016-005.

9.10 We recommended and the LCE agreed to instruct the a) Accounting Office
to coordinate with the Municipal Assessor’s Office for the proper set-up,
maintenance, and updating of the SLs and record necessary adjusting entries, if
any; and b) Assessor’s Office to conduct collection campaign through sending of
reminder/demand letters to the delinquent beneficiaries.

10.5 We recommended and Management agreed that (a) GSO and Inventory
Committee (i) conduct the periodic physical count of inventories and (ii) prepare
and submit the RPCI to the Audit Team; (b) GSO maintain the SCs to record
receipts and issuances of supplies; and (c) Municipal Accountant maintain the SLCs
for each type of supplies.

11.5 We recommended and the LCE agreed to instruct the:
a) Inventory Committee to submit the complete RPCPPE, including CIP
accounts, not later than January 31 of each year;
b) GSO to (i) maintain Local Road Inventory and Road Map, in
coordination with the MEO; and (ii) keep Local Road Network Property
Card;
c) Concerned Offices to secure documents to establish the transfer/donation
to the Municipality of the donated properties; and
d) Municipal Accountant to (i) maintain subsidiary records for roads and
road components for every road network; (ii) henceforth, disclose in the
Notes to FS the total road system; and (iii) recognize the donated
properties in the books of accounts.

12.15 We recommended and Management, headed by the LCE, agreed to continue
to: (i) ensure optimal use of the DF so that socio-economic programs and projects
that are beneficial to the LGU’s constituents are timely implemented; and (ii)
comply with the rules on the appropriation and utilization of the 20% DF for
eligible projects pursuant to Item 3.2.5 of the DBM-DOF-DILG JMC No. 1 dated
November 4, 2020.
12.16 We reiterated the audit recommendation and the LCE agreed to direct the
Municipal Planning and Development Office (MPDO) and Municipal Budget Office
(MBO) to assist the Municipal Development Council in adhering to the guidelines
set by COA MC No. 95-216 in preparing the AIP and Annual Budget.

13.14 We recommended and the LCE agreed to direct the (a) the Bids and Awards
Committee (BAC) to strictly follow the provisions of the IRR of R.A. No. 9184 in the
procurement of goods and services; and (b) Municipal Accountant to check the
completeness of supporting documents provided by COA Circular 2012-001 before
effecting payment for contracted projects. We further recommend the submission of
the revised AIP as required by NDRRMC, DBM, and DILG JMC 2013-1 dated
March 25, 2013.

14.9 We recommended and Management agreed to instruct the (a) GFPS to (i)
update the Municipality’s GAD Database; (ii) submit in writing the changes in the
DILG-approved GPB and secure an acknowledgment receipt of adjusted GPB from
the DILG to ensure the compliance of their adjustments to the requirements of the
JMCs; and (iii) implement the use of the HGDG and PIMME tools to measure the
actual cost attributed to gender-responsiveness and know if the existing mechanism
actually responds to the basic 5% requirement of the law; and (b) Municipal
Accountant submit to the Audit Team the DVs pertaining to the noted transactions
above.

15.11 We recommended and the LCE agreed to direct the Municipal Treasurer to
(i) continue to comply and enforce the Municipal Revenue Code so that correct
business tax will accrue for the benefit of the Municipality; and (ii) require the
submission of a sworn statement of the business’ gross sales/receipt and copy of
income tax return to ensure that the basis declared by the taxpayers are reliable and
accurate.

16.11 We recommended and Management agreed to make representation or send
communication to the DENR-EMB regarding the status or condition of the
transferred equipment and facility to the Municipality in order to undertake
necessary measures, either to repair or return them, to stop the risks of their further
deterioration and wastage of government funds.
16.12 We also recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to record the
biodegradable waste shredder and composter totaling ₱1,005,000.00, which were
transferred to the Municipality, in the books of accounts.

We recommended and the Local Chief Executive (LCE) agreed to instruct the: (a) Inventory Committee, MDRRMO, MSWDO, and Municipal Accountant to determine the actual Medical, Dental and Laboratory supplies and Welfare Goods consumed and/or distributed during the year, make the necessary adjusting entries and properly account for the remaining items on hand if any; and (b) Bids and Not Awards Committee (BAC) and concerned officials of the agency to comply with the requirements under the 2016 Revised IRR of R.A. No. 9184 and Government Procurement Policy Board (GPPB) Circular No. 2020-001 and submit the lacking documentary requirements to the Audit Team so that transactions that were not yet passed in audit may undergo further evaluation.

We recommended and the Management agreed that the: (a) Municipal Accountant submit to the Audit Team the DVs pertaining to the noted transactions; (b) the Accounting and the Treasury Offices adhere to the general principles of imprest fund system that all payments, particularly on procured goods, should be paid through check in the name of the supplier and limit the practice of paying claims through reimbursement; (c) Municipal Accountant and BAC comply with the requirements under the 2016 revised IRR of R.A. No. 9184 and submit the lacking documentary requirements to the Audit Team; and (d) GAD Focal Point System (GFPS) prioritize the adoption of the HGDG and PIMME tools to measure the actual cost attributed to gender-responsiveness and know if the existing mechanism actually responds to the basic 5% requirement of the law.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and the LCE agreed to instruct the Municipal Accountant to ensure proper classification of Loans Payable in the Statement of Financial Position by presenting the portion due to be settled within 12 months after the reporting date as Current Liabilities and the remaining portion as Non-Current Liabilities.

We recommended and the Management agreed that the: (a) members of the Local Finance Committee (LFC) continue to ensure that the required appropriation for local development fund is always observed; (b) Municipal Development Council: (i) continue to comply with the guidelines mandated by DBM-DOF-DILG JMC No. 2020-1 in the utilization of the 20% DF; and (ii) adhere to the guidelines set by COA MC No. 95-216 in the preparation of the AIP and Annual Budget; and (c) BAC Secretariat/Concerned Officials submit copies of contracts entered into by the Municipality and/or issued POs within five (5) days from execution thereof in consonance with COA Circular No. 2009-01.

We recommended and the Management agreed to return the unused balance of fund transfers to the SAs in compliance with Section 6.7 of COA Circular No. 94-013 dated December 13, 1994. Further, we recommended and LCE agreed to instruct the Accounting Office, Budget Officer and other concerned Offices to exhaust all means to locate documents pertaining to the dormant fund transfers, determine whether the purposes can still be achieved, and consider the write-off of dormant accounts in accordance with COA Circular No. 2016-005.

We recommended and the Management agreed to post the Reports on Fund Utilization and Status of Program/Project Implementation for the Local Government Support Fund using the prescribed format in at least three (3) conspicuous public places in the locality and on the LGU’s website within 20 calendar days from the end of each quarter in compliance with Item 6.3 of DBM LBC No. 142 dated January 24, 2022.

We recommended and the Management agreed to instruct the Bids Awards Committee (BAC) and concerned officials of the agency to comply with the requirements under the 2016 Revised IRR of R.A. No. 9184 and GPPB Circular No. 2020-001 and submit the lacking documentary requirements to the Audit Team so that transactions that were not yet cleared in audit may undergo further evaluation.

We reiterated our previous recommendations and the Management agreed to instruct the (i) Municipal Accountant to exhaust all remedies to gather the supporting documents for the receivables noted and consider the write-off of dormant accounts; and (ii) Accounting Office and HRMO to require Dr. Frias to immediately liquidate his cash advances by refunding the amount pursuant to the directive of the Supreme Court.

We reiterated our previous recommendations and the Management agreed to instruct the Accounting Office (i) to coordinate with the Municipal Social Welfare and Development Office (MSWDO) for the establishment of the collectability of the receivables and record any necessary adjustments; and (ii) to consider the write-off of dormant accounts in accordance with the procedures set forth in COA Circular No. 2016-005.

We recommended and the Management agreed that the (a) MGSO together with the Inventory Committee complete the conduct of physical count covering all classes of properties owned by the  Municipality; and (b) Municipal Accountant and the MGSO (i) prepare and maintain required records and reports of  all PPE accounts and reconcile the same; and (ii) determine the cost or fair market value of fixed assets leased out under one or more operating leases on a commercial basis to external parties to facilitate their reclassification from PPE to investment property.

We recommended and the Management agreed to require the (a) Municipal Development Council to adhere to the guidelines set by COA Memorandum Circular No. 95-216 in the preparation of the AIP and Annual Budget; (b) BAC Secretariat/concerned officials to submit copies of contracts entered into by the Municipality and/or issued POs within five days from execution thereof in consonance with COA Circular No. 2009-01; and (c) BAC to submit the lacking documents mentioned in the report and observe full compliance with the provisions of IRR of R.A. No. 9184 and COA Circular No. 2012-001.

We recommended and the LCE agreed to instruct (a) all concerned officials and personnel (i) to strictly adhere to the provisions concerning the implementation of projects funded by LGSF; and (ii) to submit the lacking documents to the Audit Team for evaluation and examination and observe stringent compliance on the submission of documentary requirements of transactions; (b) the Municipal Accountant to review thoroughly all claims presented for payment prior to approval to ensure that only properly and completely documented transactions/disbursement are paid;  and (c) the Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Accountant to immediately return to the National Treasury the unutilized fund from the LGSF as of December 31, 2021.

We recommended and the Management agreed to instruct the: (a) Inventory Committee, MDRRMO and OIC–Municipal Accountant to determine the actual Medical, Dental and Laboratory supplies and Welfare Goods consumed and/or distributed during the year, make the necessary adjusting entries and properly account for the remaining items on hand, if any; and (b) BAC and concerned officials of the agency to comply with the requirements under the 2016 Revised IRR of R.A. No. 9184 and submit the lacking documentary requirements to the Audit Team so that transactions that were not yet cleared in audit may undergo further evaluation.

We recommended and the Management agreed that the GAD Focal Point System prioritize the adoption of the HGDG and PIMME tools to measure the actual cost attributed to gender-responsiveness and know if the existing mechanism actually responds to the basic 5% requirement of the law.

We recommended and Management agreed to:

Direct the Bids and Awards Committee and /or BAC Secretariat to submit the deficient documents in accordance with above-cited provisions of DILG Memorandum Circular (MC) 2019-71 dated May 20, 2019; and

 instruct the concerned officials to ensure compliance with specific guidelines in implementing LGSF-AM projects such as maintenance of documents, insertion of the required provision of the approved contract and installation of DILG Community Billboard containing all the DILG implemented projects.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct the Accounting and Treasury Offices to:
record necessary adjusting entries disclosed in the preparation of year-end BRS;
prepare procedures for direct deposit mode of payments of taxpayers wherein the payments will be captured and recognized by the said Offices on time, and to disseminate the same to the concerned payors; and
comply with the requirements of the /LGAM on the adjustment of unreleased checks as at year-end.

We reiterated the audit recommendation that the:
Municipal Accountant exhaust all remedies to gather the supporting documents for the receivables noted and consider the write-off of dormant accounts; and
Accounting Office and Human Resource Office require Dr. Frias to immediately liquidate his cash advances either by presenting liquidation documents or by refunding the unsettled cash advances.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive:
instruct the Accounting Office to coordinate with the Municipal Assessor’s Office for the proper set-up, maintenance and updating of the SLs and record necessary adjusting entry, if any;
direct the Assessor’s Office to submit copies of agreements entered into by the Municipality with the land owners and the beneficiaries to the Audit Team; and
rename the project/program with a more appropriate one, instead of ‘Low-Cost Housing’ so as not to mislead financial report users.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct the Accounting Office:
to coordinate with the Municipal Social Welfare and Development Office (MSWDO) for the establishment of the collectability of the receivables and record any necessary adjustments; and
to consider the write-off of dormant accounts in accordance with the procedures set forth by COA Circular No. 2016-005.

We reiterated the audit recommendation that the GSO conduct a complete annual physical count of PPEs and come up with RPCPPE with its related reconciliation and maintain property cards.
We further recommended that the Accounting Office:
coordinate with the Assessor’s Office regarding the parcels of land the Municipality owns for proper recognition and/or reporting; and
trace disbursements relative to lot procurement and record necessary adjustments in the books accounts.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the -
BAC, BAC Secretariat and Accounting Office to submit the lacking documents to the Audit Team and observe compliance to the provisions of GPPB Circular on emergency procurement and pertinent provisions of R.A. No. 9184 on procurement thru public bidding;
GSO, MDRRMO, MSWDO and Accounting Office to comply with the provisions of COA Circular No. 2014-002 and the Government Accounting Manual for LGUs; and
MSWDO to submit the claimed RIS and stock cards on the procured rice to the Audit Team for its evaluation and further audit actions.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the -
BAC, BAC Secretariat and Accounting Office to submit the lacking documents to the Audit Team and observe compliance to the provisions of GPPB Circular on emergency procurement and pertinent provisions of R.A. No. 9184 on procurement thru public bidding;
GSO, MDRRMO, MSWDO and Accounting Office to comply with the provisions of COA Circular No. 2014-002 and the Government Accounting Manual for LGUs; and
MSWDO to submit the claimed RIS and stock cards on the procured rice to the Audit Team for its evaluation and further audit actions.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the concerned Officials to:
be informed of the reportorial and monitoring requirement as mentioned in COA Circular No. 2020-009, in relation to COA Circular No. 2014-002;
strategize the receipt and distribution of donations-in-kind by identifying Officers who will be responsible in the preparation of acknowledgement receipts and will turn over donated items to the supply/property officer; and
